the oil industry we typically plot a bubble plot at each well location on a map where the size of the bubble might be scaled by daily oil production. In our applications the bubbles are actually pie charts at each well location where the pie could be divided up by total oil, water or gas production at each well.

I would think that this application might be useful for other mapping applications too. The following is an example created in Spotfire. 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e3ba612d-3504-4cb2-957c-787197586007)

Problem description

I want to plot bubble maps with pie charts as the face of each bubble. 

### Feature description

Hello, I use Bokeh and Pane for all of my interactive python. I would like to make a bubble map for each well in an oil field where the size of the bubble relates to total oil production and bubble is actually a pie chart showing total oil and water production at each well. 



### Potential alternatives

I really do not have an alternative because what I am proposing is the norm in my industry. 

### Additional information

In the example below the red piece of the pie chart is oil production and the blue piece is water production. 


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/79f281c3-65b3-4df6-af16-4ea9b3ce8c36)

This is plotting just as I want:


![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ace6283e-0040-4397-a10b-e703771efe35)

and this is my code:

`from bokeh.plotting import figure, show, output_notebook
from bokeh.models import ColumnDataSource
import numpy as np

# Enable Bokeh output in the notebook
output_notebook()

# Extract relevant columns and group by well, summing the production
grouped_data = (
    df[['Well', 'BOPM', 'BWPM', 'X_location', 'Y_location']]
    .dropna()
    .groupby(['Well', 'X_location', 'Y_location'], as_index=False)
    .sum()
)

# Calculate total production and proportions
grouped_data['Total'] = grouped_data['BOPM'] + grouped_data['BWPM']
grouped_data['BOPM_Fraction'] = grouped_data['BOPM'] / grouped_data['Total']
grouped_data['BWPM_Fraction'] = grouped_data['BWPM'] / grouped_data['Total']

# Precompute angles for the wedges
grouped_data['start_angle_oil'] = 0
grouped_data['end_angle_oil'] = grouped_data['BOPM_Fraction'] * 2 * np.pi
grouped_data['start_angle_water'] = grouped_data['end_angle_oil']
grouped_data['end_angle_water'] = 2 * np.pi

# Scale the radius based on BOPM
max_radius = 0.03  # Maximum radius for the largest pie
grouped_data['radius'] = grouped_data['BOPM'] / grouped_data['BOPM'].max() * max_radius

# Convert to a ColumnDataSource
source = ColumnDataSource(grouped_data)

# Create the figure
p = figure(title="Sized Well Production with Pie Charts", width=800, height=600, x_range=(0, 1), y_range=(0, 1))

# Add oil wedges
p.wedge(
    x='X_location',
    y='Y_location',
    radius='radius',
    start_angle='start_angle_oil',
    end_angle='end_angle_oil',
    color="red",
    legend_label="Oil",
    source=source
)

# Add water wedges
p.wedge(
    x='X_location',
    y='Y_location',
    radius='radius',
    start_angle='start_angle_water',
    end_angle='end_angle_water',
    color="blue",
    legend_label="Water",
    source=source
)

# Display the chart
show(p)
`

I am not sure what I am doing wrong. The following is the simple code for this example where cbw_int_plot is a matplotlib plot shown below: CBW_Int_slider = pn.widgets.FloatSlider(name='CBW_Intercept',start=0,end=0.5,step=0.01,value=0.25) pn.interact(cbw_int_plot, CBW_Int = CBW_Int_slider) Do you have any suggestions? This is the plot definition. def cbw_int_plot(CBW_Int): fig=plt.figure(figsize=(5,4)) plt.title('Vsh vs.CBWa', color = 'blue') plt.plot(logs.vsh,logs.CBWa,'r.', label='',color='red') plt.plot(logs.vsh,logs.vsh*CBW_Int,'k-', label='',color='black') plt.xlim(0.0,1.0) plt.ylim(0.0,1.0) plt.ylabel('CBWa [v/v]', color = 'blue') plt.xlabel('Vsh [v/v]', color = 'blue') return fig ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/4952/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/4952/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

I tried you suggestion and I now have a single plot, but I want an interactive figure too. I tried to add the widget using the following code, but that did not work. Is there any way to add the widget to this: from matplotlib.figure import Figure fig=plt.Figure(figsize=(5,4)) plt.title('Vsh vs.CBWa', color = 'blue') plt.plot(logs.vsh,logs.CBWa,'r.', label='',color='red') plt.plot(logs.vsh,logs.vsh*CBW_Int,'k-', label='',color='black') plt.xlim(0.0,1) plt.ylim(0.0,1.0) plt.ylabel('CBWa [v/v]', color = 'blue') plt.xlabel('Vsh [v/v]', color = 'blue') plt.grid() CBW_Int_slider = pn.widgets.FloatSlider(name='CBW_Intercept',start=0,end=0.5,step=0.01,value=0.25) test = pn.pane.Matplotlib(fig) pn.interact(test, CBW_Int = CBW_Int_slider) _TypeError: unsupported callable_ I then tried: def cbw_int_plot(CBW_Int): fig=plt.Figure(figsize=(5,4)) plt.title('Vsh vs.CBWa', color = 'blue') plt.plot(logs.vsh,logs.CBWa,'r.', label='',color='red') plt.plot(logs.vsh,logs.vsh*CBW_Int,'k-', label='',color='black') plt.xlim(0.0,1) plt.ylim(0.0,1.0) plt.ylabel('CBWa [v/v]', color = 'blue') plt.xlabel('Vsh [v/v]', color = 'blue') plt.grid() pn.pane.Matplotlib(fig) CBW_Int_slider = pn.widgets.FloatSlider(name='CBW_Intercept',start=0,end=0.5,step=0.01,value=0.25) pn.interact(cbw_int_plot, CBW_Int = CBW_Int_slider) This looks great with 1 plot, but that plot does now interact with the slide bar.I guess for my Jupyter Notebook I am best staying with ipywidgets and in my other application, that is not a notebook, then use panel. I just like to use the Notebook as a documented help type file for the primary application, and I would love to use the same code on both. is another way to run this interactive plot by using panel server, but then it spews out a long series of my interactive plots at the end after saving the value of the widget. Is there any way to suppress these extra plots created while saving the value (CBW_Int = CBW_Int_slider.value) from the slider? CBW_Int_slider = pn.widgets.FloatSlider(name='CBW_Intercept',start=0,end=0.5,step=0.01,value=0.25) pane = pn.interact(cbw_int_plot, CBW_Int = CBW_Int_slider) # Button to stop the server def button_callback(): #sys.exit() # Stop the server print('Panel Stop') bokeh_server.stop() button = Button(label="Stop", button_type="success") button.on_click(button_callback) def app(): return pn.Row(pn.Column( button, pane[0], width=350, sizing_mode="fixed"), pn.layout.VSpacer(width=10),pane[1]) pn.serve({"localhost:": app}, port=5006) ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/holoviz/panel/issues/comments/1565494413/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
